decision.decision: DROP MAR from the inc-07 exact-process baseline; build the legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing-note write path instead; park MAR as CR-07-MAR for owner sign-off. VERIFIED against code: legacy PatientPrescriptionChart.java:34-82 has ONLY dosage + output + remark (all free-text) + FKs (prescription, encounter, patient, nurse, clinician) + createdAt (record-creation timestamp, NOT a dose-administration time). There is NO administeredAt, NO routeUid, NO doseGiven, NO patientResponse, and NO dispensedAt field anywhere in the legacy Zana codebase (grep across ZANAHMIS-2-feature returns zero matches) — so both the MAR aggregate (M15) and its stated rationale (M15: "prior build tracked only dispensedAt") are confirmed phantom. CONCRETE BUILD: in 07b (nursing-charts), wire the deferred write path on the already-mapped HMSCLEAN2 entity (clinical/domain/PatientPrescriptionChart.java) reproducing savePatientPrescriptionChart (PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590) exactly: (a) nurse uid required; (b) linked prescription status MUST be GIVEN else reject "Prescription not picked from pharmacy" (:2544); (c) exactly-one encounter binding (consultation/nonConsultation/admissionUid) — and for the inpatient path reject consultation ("Operation not available for outpatients") and nonConsultation ("...for outsiders") so the inpatient chart is admission-bound; (d) admission MUST be IN-PROCESS — reject PENDING ("Admission not verified") and signed-off ("Patient already signed off") (:2570-2577); (e) copy patient + nurse + createdBy/createdOn/createdAt. 07a/07b/07c MUST NOT build any MedicationAdministration aggregate, MAR table, routeUid/administeredAt/doseGiven/patientResponse columns, route masterdata, or any closed-loop administration endpoint in the exact-process baseline.
decision.rationale: A closed-loop MAR is genuinely better clinical-safety practice (TFDA/MOH medication-administration documentation, the five-rights, adverse-reaction capture), but it is exactly the "tempting improvement with no legacy basis" the charter governs: the legacy never implemented it and the planning-doc premise that justified it (dispensedAt tracking) is factually wrong. Under "MODERN DESIGN, EXACT PROCESS," the non-negotiable baseline is therefore to reproduce the legacy free-text dosing-note process, and any departure (a structured MAR) is a deliberate DEVIATION requiring a written, APPROVED CR BEFORE implementation. I am NOT silently building MAR into the baseline, and I am NOT inventing a clinical workflow beyond what the legacy implements. No ADR speaks to medication administration, so this is not ADR-pre-decided; it has clear process impact, so it is not a pure architecture-placement decision. The classification is DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR. (Note: the go/no-go on funding/adopting MAR as a safety feature is genuinely an owner sign-off and is captured under ownerActionRequired, but that sign-off does not alter the baseline I am setting here — hence the primary classification stays DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR, not OWNER_RELEASE_GATE, because the baseline build is fully decidable now.)
decision.adrCitation: none — ADRs silent on medication administration / MAR. No ratified or proposed ADR addresses inpatient drug-administration documentation; ADR-0017 (concurrency) and ADR-0018 (jobs/accrual) are unrelated. The governing rule is the engagement charter ("exact process; deviations need an approved CR"), not an ADR.
decision.crNeeded: CR-07-MAR: Add a net-new closed-loop MedicationAdministration/MAR aggregate (routeUid, administeredAt, doseGiven, patientResponse) as a clinical-safety enhancement over the legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing note — owner sign-off + clinical-safety justification required before any implementation.

challenge.reason: Verified against code on all three challenge axes; the decision is correct and does not smuggle a deviation. AXIS 1 (ADR): No ratified or proposed ADR addresses medication administration / MAR. ADR grep hits are incidental — ADR-0004:18 names "inpatient MAR" only as a UI-density (density:-2) illustration, not a build mandate; the rest are PHI-logging/attachment/etc. The decision's adrCitation:"none" is accurate and it correctly does NOT claim ADR_PRE_DECIDED, so the inc-08 ADR-0017/0018 re-litigation trap does not fire. AXIS 2 (smuggled deviation): The MAR aggregate is the canonical phantom. Independently confirmed zero matches for administeredAt/routeUid/doseGiven/patientResponse/dispensedAt/MedicationAdministration across the entire legacy ZANAHMIS-2-feature codebase — so BOTH the M15 aggregate AND its stated rationale ("prior build tracked only dispensedAt") are phantom. The phantom originates in docs/delivery/increments/07-inpatient-nursing.md (line 21 aggregate spec, line 88 false M15 premise, lines 41-42/74/108/116-117 endpoints+DoD). The decision correctly refuses to build MAR into the exact-process baseline, parks it as CR-07-MAR, and instead builds the verified-exact legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart write path. Every rule in the build-spec matches PatientServiceImpl.savePatientPrescriptionChart (lines 2533-2590): nurse required (2536-2538); GIVEN guard "Prescription not picked from pharmacy" (2544-2546); exactly-one encounter binding (2549-2562); reject consultation "Operation not available for outpatients" (2564-2565); reject nonConsultation "Operation not available for outsiders" (2567-2568); admission PENDING->"Admission not verified" / IN-PROCESS->continue / else->"Patient already signed off" (2570-2577); copy patient+nurse+createdBy/On/At (2579-2587). The HMSCLEAN2 entity (backend/.../clinical/domain/PatientPrescriptionChart.java) carries only free-text dosage/output/remark + FKs + auditable fields — no MAR columns. The read surface and 24h delete window the decision lists are ALSO legacy-grounded (PatientResource.java:2882 prescription_charts_by_id read; PatientResource.java:3117-3120 ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(...)>=24 delete rejection), not invented controls. The decision does not touch the other inc-07 watch-list items (ward-accrual cron, pessimistic lock, second-approver gate, deceased admit-guard, consumables-from-stock, WardTransfer, new ServiceKind, ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata, *-WRITE/*-DISCHARGE privilege codes). AXIS 3 (classification/owner action): DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR is correct — the baseline build is fully decidable now (reproduce the legacy dosing note), so it is not OWNER_RELEASE_GATE; the MAR go/no-go is surfaced under ownerActionRequired (engagement-lead + HDE, with data-architect/security-architect follow-ons) and is not silently decided or pre-funded. The CR-07-MAR scope and the "MUST NOT BUILD" fence (no MAR entity/table, no routeUid/administeredAt/doseGiven/patientResponse columns, no route masterdata, no administer/closed-loop endpoint, no MAR-driven stock decrement; MAR stays a net-new acceptance test only, not golden-master parity) are all correct.
challenge.correction: No correction required to the classification, build-spec, or owner-action flagging — UPHELD as written. One non-blocking advisory wording-precision note for the 07b build of record: the decision says "24h edit/delete window," but the legacy enforces the 24-hour window on DELETE only (PatientResource.java:3117-3120 deletePrescriptionChart, ">=24" rejects with "Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted"). decision.decision: The ward-accrual PARITY BASELINE (the legacy business result inc-07 must reproduce) is fixed and buildable-as-specification now: total ward charge = (1 + N) x WardType.price where N = the number of completed rolling-24h intervals from AdmissionBed.openedAt while the admission is IN-PROCESS or STOPPED; accrual STOPS the instant status leaves IN-PROCESS/STOPPED (SIGNED-OUT/HELD); the admission-time bill is UNPAID, each accrued bill is VERIFIED, and an insurance-eligible bill is COVERED; insurance ward price resolves via WardTypeInsurancePlan max-price/exact-plan match with a UNPAID "Top up" supplementary bill for the (WardType.price - coveredPrice) difference (cite legacy UpdatePatient.java:258-334, PatientServiceImpl.java:1753-1897). The MECHANISM (@Scheduled + ShedLock midnight cron, per-calendar-night idempotency on (admission_uid, business_date)) is NET-NEW and is PARKED behind an OWNER_RELEASE_GATE: it cannot be built until the engagement-lead RATIFIES ADR-0018 (currently Proposed) AND authorizes net-new scheduling infrastructure (ShedLock dependency, shedlock + job_run_log Flyway tables, @EnableScheduling). Two owner-only items must land before 07c codes the accrual job: (1) ratify ADR-0018, and (2) accept the documented midnight-cron-vs-rolling-24h TIMING VARIANCE as a tolerated deviation OR require true elapsed-24h parity. The golden-master for 07c MUST reconcile against the legacy elapsed-24h-from-openedAt total, not assume a calendar-night count; any residual per-night-vs-per-24h count difference for stays crossing midnight is recorded as a known, owner-accepted variance.
decision.rationale: Charter test applied strictly. (1) ADR_PRE_DECIDED is excluded because ratification is unverified-and-false: docs/adr/README.md line 3 and ADR-0018 line 3 both carry status "Proposed", so the no-re-litigate rule does not fire and the ADR target becomes owner-gated (same posture inc-08 took for CR-08-Q4 / NULLS-LAST). (2) The mechanism is unambiguously NET-NEW by the ADR's own words: ADR-0018 §Exact-process-impact (lines 130-132) calls JOB-001 "a deliberate process improvement, not a divergence," and grep confirms ZERO scheduling infra in both legacy (ZANAHMIS-2-feature) and HMSCLEAN2 (no @Scheduled/ShedLock/Quartz match). (3) But this is not a free DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR that the architect resolves unilaterally — the cron requires (a) ratifying a Proposed ADR and (b) adding a dependency + scheduling infra + accepting a timing variance on a money-bearing financial accrual. Those are owner-only sign-offs (engagement-lead + data-architect per Q2's named owners), so the correct classification is OWNER_RELEASE_GATE: I specify what parity the build must hit and what infra the cron needs, but I do not pre-decide the owner's ratification. (4) ADR-0018's stated premise is factually WRONG and must be corrected upstream before ratification: it asserts (lines 15, 130) "legacy charges one flat ward-bed bill at admission and does not re-accrue," but legacy DOES re-accrue per rolling-24h via the UpdatePatient polling Thread (UpdatePatient.java:258-299, started at MainApplication.java:160-184). The accrual TOTAL/STATUS semantics are therefore parity facts, not net-new — only the trigger mechanism and per-calendar-night idempotency model are net-new. This correction protects the golden-master from being anchored to a false "one flat charge" baseline.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0018 (Background Jobs and Scheduling) — STATUS PROPOSED, not ratified (README.md:3, ADR-0018:3). JOB-001 ward-day accrual cron is the governing item. ADR-0018 §Exact-process-impact (lines 130-132) self-labels it "a deliberate process improvement, not a divergence." ADR-0018 §Context (line 15) and §Exact-process-impact (line 130) contain a FACTUAL ERROR ("legacy charges one flat amount and does not re-accrue") that must be corrected before ratification. Related: ADR-0009 §money (2dp accrual), ADR-0007 (job-run audit). Charter: a ratification-needing ADR target is OWNER_RELEASE_GATE, not ADR_PRE_DECIDED.
decision.crNeeded: CR-07-Q2: Adopt net-new @Scheduled+ShedLock ward-day accrual cron (calendar-night model) in place of the legacy rolling-24h-from-openedAt polling Thread, accepting the documented midnight-vs-24h timing variance — pending ADR-0018 ratification + scheduling-infra authorization.
decision.buildImpact: 07a (Admission+Discharge+Ward) MUST build now: AdmissionBed ledger (status OPENED/CLOSED, openedAt/closedAt, @OneToOne PatientBill); the admission-time UNPAID ward-bed bill priced at WardType.price (qty=1); WardTypeInsurancePlan max-price/exact-match resolution + the UNPAID "Top up" supplementary bill; the hard bills-cleared discharge gate (UNPAID+VERIFIED both block). 07a MUST NOT close AdmissionBed at discharge unless Q10 approves a CR (legacy leaves the final AdmissionBed OPENED). 07c (Consumable + ward accrual) MUST build the legacy-faithful accrual TOTAL/STATUS semantics — (1+N) x WardType.price, first bill UNPAID, accrued VERIFIED, insurance COVERED, accrual halts when status leaves IN-PROCESS/STOPPED — as the parity oracle. 07c MUST NOT build the @Scheduled+ShedLock cron, the shedlock/job_run_log Flyway tables, @EnableScheduling, or the (admission_uid, business_date) ON CONFLICT idempotency key UNTIL the owner ratifies ADR-0018 and authorizes scheduling infra. Until then 07c may stage the accrual SERVICE METHOD (accrueWardDay, independent-tx, idempotent) callable via the manual POST /ops/jobs trigger, but no scheduler binding. The golden-master harness MUST compute expected totals from elapsed-24h-from-openedAt, not calendar nights, and assert the documented variance band.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement-lead + data-architect (the Q2-named owners) must: (1) RATIFY ADR-0018 (status Proposed) — but only AFTER the solution-architect issues the upstream correction to ADR-0018 §Context/§Exact-process-impact replacing the false "one flat charge, does not re-accrue" premise with the verified rolling-24h re-accrual fact; (2) AUTHORIZE net-new scheduling infrastructure (ShedLock dependency, shedlock + job_run_log Flyway migrations, @EnableScheduling) as an architecture/dependency addition; (3) DECIDE the parity bar: accept the midnight-cron-vs-rolling-24h timing variance as a tolerated, runbook-documented deviation (approving CR-07-Q2) OR require true elapsed-24h parity (forcing a rolling-interval accrual design rather than a calendar-night cron). None of these may be decided by the architect; 07c's accrual scheduler stays parked until all three land.

challenge.reason:
Verified against source on all three challenge axes; the resolution holds.

AXIS 1 (ADR contradiction / false ADR_PRE_DECIDED): CLEAN. The specialist classified Q2 as OWNER_RELEASE_GATE and did NOT claim ADR_PRE_DECIDED. Confirmed docs/adr/README.md:3 and docs/adr/0018-background-jobs-scheduling.md:3 both carry status "Proposed" — the no-re-litigate rule does not fire, so the ratification-needing ADR target is correctly owner-gated. This is the inc-08 ADR-0017/0018 lesson applied exactly (same posture as CR-08-Q4 / NULLS-LAST). ADR-0017 and ADR-0007 also confirmed "Proposed"; nothing ratified contradicts the classification.

AXIS 2 (smuggled deviation without CR): CLEAN. The net-new cron mechanism (@Scheduled + ShedLock + shedlock/job_run_log Flyway tables + @EnableScheduling + (admission_uid, business_date) calendar-night idempotency) is explicitly PARKED behind the owner gate AND behind CR-07-Q2 — not silently built. Confirmed ZERO scheduling infra in HMSCLEAN2: grep for @Scheduled/@EnableScheduling/@SchedulerLock/net.javacrumbs.shedlock across *.java and *.xml returned no matches (all hits are in docs/ADRs/discovery only). The accrual SERVICE METHOD may be staged with no scheduler binding — acceptable.

CRITICAL FACT-CHECK SUSTAINED: The specialist's claim that ADR-0018's premise is FACTUALLY WRONG is correct and decisive. ADR-0018:15 and :130-132 assert "legacy charges one flat ward-bed bill at admission and does not re-accrue." Legacy code disproves this: UpdatePatient.java (a Runnable, line 72) polls every 5 minutes (line 110) and for each IN-PROCESS/STOPPED admission (lines 258-262), when ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now) >= 24 (lines 291-292), it CLOSES the current AdmissionBed and OPENS a new one with openedAt=now (lines 294-334) and creates a new VERIFIED ward-bed PatientBill at WardType.price (lines 304-325). The thread is started at MainApplication.java:160-184. So legacy DOES re-accrue per chained rolling-24h. The parity baseline ((1+N) x WardType.price; admission-time bill UNPAID per PatientServiceImpl.java:1760; accrued bills VERIFIED per UpdatePatient.java:311; insurance COVERED per :365 and PatientServiceImpl.java:1818; WardTypeInsurancePlan max-price/exact-match resolution + UNPAID "Top up" supplementary bill per :420-490 and PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1897; accrual confined to IN-PROCESS/STOPPED per :258-262) is genuine EXTRACTED legacy behaviour, not invention. The specialist correctly separated parity facts (total/status semantics) from net-new (trigger mechanism + per-calendar-night idempotency model) and routed the ADR-0018 upstream correction to the solution-architect before ratification. No WARD_DAY ServiceKind is introduced (HMSCLEAN2 ServiceKind.java:24,47-51 keeps WARD only; legacy reuses WardType.price per accrued bill), so no new-ServiceKind deviation is smuggled.

AXIS 3 (classification & owner action): CORRECT. OWNER_RELEASE_GATE is the right call. The three owner-only items (ratify ADR-0018; authorize scheduling infra; decide the midnight-cron-vs-rolling-24h timing-variance parity bar) are flagged for engagement-lead + data-architect and NOT pre-decided by the architect. CR-07-Q2 is correctly scoped as the calendar-night-vs-rolling-24h deviation.
challenge.correction:
No REVISE required — resolution is upheld as written. Two ADVISORY (non-blocking) refinements for 07c implementation:

(1) GOLDEN-MASTER ORACLE PRECISION: The instruction "compute expected totals from elapsed-24h-from-openedAt" slightly under-specifies the legacy mechanic. Legacy does NOT compute floor(elapsed_since_original_openedAt / 24h). It chains RESETTING 24h windows: each accrual sets the new AdmissionBed.openedAt = now (UpdatePatient.java:333), and the next accrual fires only when 24h elapses from that reset point, evaluated at a 5-minute poll cadence (Thread.sleep 300000, line 110). With non-zero poll latency the chained count N can be strictly LOWER than a single elapsed/24 division for long stays. The harness should model the chained-reset-plus-poll-latency accrual (or assert a variance band that bounds it) rather than a single division, so the oracle is not falsely tight. The specialist's "documented variance band" language already anticipates this; this note just pins the exact mechanic.

(2) DISCHARGE-GATE SCOPE BLEED: buildImpact states the 07a item "the hard bills-cleared discharge gate (UNPAID+VERIFIED both block)" as fact. That is a legacy DISCHARGE behaviour claim belonging to Q10's scope, and it is asserted here without a pinpoint citation to the legacy discharge method (discharge logic was not located in PatientServiceImpl under that name; it lives in PatientResource/elsewhere). Keep this Q2 resolution's authority confined to the ACCRUAL total/status semantics it actually evidenced (UpdatePatient.java:262-547, PatientServiceImpl.java:1753-1897); the discharge-gate and AdmissionBed-close-at-discharge behaviour must be sourced and adjudicated under Q10 with its own legacy citation, exactly as the resolution already says 07a "MUST NOT close AdmissionBed at discharge unless Q10 approves a CR." Do not let the unsourced "both block" phrasing harden into a fidelity baseline before Q10's legacy extraction confirms it.

Neither item changes the parity baseline, the OWNER_RELEASE_GATE classification, the CR-07-Q2 parking, or the ADR-0018 upstream-correction routing. decision.decision: Inherit inc-08's parked @Version-only posture for the admission / bed / ward-bill aggregate. The bed-claim race (two admits to the same EMPTY WardBed) is handled in the exact-process baseline by the shared AuditableEntity @Version optimistic lock: both admits load the same EMPTY WardBed row, the first commit wins (EMPTY->WAITING), the second commit raises OptimisticLockException surfaced as 409 STALE_ENTITY via the ProblemDetail handler (urn:hmis:error:stale-entity, ADR-0009). This is strictly safer than legacy (legacy had NO lock and NO @Version on beds at all) and reproduces the legacy WardBed cycle EMPTY->WAITING->OCCUPIED->EMPTY exactly. Lifting to a PESSIMISTIC_WRITE row lock on the bed/admission aggregate is NOT decided here — it is the inc-07 instance of the same gate inc-08 raised (extending the parked CR-08-Q4 lock to a new aggregate) and requires (a) ADR-0017 ratification by the engagement lead AND (b) explicit owner approval of the deviation. Do not pre-decide it for the owner.
decision.rationale: Charter strict-application: (1) No ratified ADR pre-decides this. ADR-0017 is STATUS PROPOSED (verified 0017-concurrency-and-locking.md:2), so the no-re-litigate rule does not fire; and even if ratified, ADR-0017 §2 scopes the pessimistic lock to STOCK decrement (stock_balance + FEFO stock_batch) and document numbers — it says nothing about WardBed occupancy / admission claim, so a bed-claim lock is an EXTENSION to a new aggregate, not an ADR-0017 mandate. (2) Verified precedent: inc-08 re-classified the identical question from ADR_PRE_DECIDED to OWNER_RELEASE_GATE — 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED Q4 'Not auto-decided (ADR-0017 is Proposed, not ratified)... Parked behind CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017 ratification', and the named owner action is 'approve the lock deviation (CR-08-Q4) AND ratify ADR-0017'. inc-07 inherits this posture verbatim. (3) Verified as-built: there is NO active pessimistic lock anywhere — the only PESSIMISTIC_WRITE/@Lock matches in the codebase are Javadoc in StockService.java:36-37, StockBatchRepository.java:23, and PharmacyMedicineRepository.java:13-14 that EXPLICITLY document the lock as parked (CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017, AC-STK-10); the inherited @Version is the sole guard. (4) The optimistic @Version already correctly resolves THIS race (both admits contend the same EMPTY bed row), so there is no correctness gap forcing an emergency deviation — only a throughput/UX preference (avoiding a retry/409 on a hot bed) would justify the lift, which is exactly an owner trade-off, not an architect's unilateral call. Reproduce-legacy baseline + owner-gated deviation is the disciplined answer.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0017 (Concurrency & Locking) — STATUS PROPOSED, and §2 scopes pessimistic locking to stock_balance/stock_batch decrement + document numbers, NOT bed/admission occupancy; §1 'optimistic locking by default' + @Version/409 STALE_ENTITY (with ADR-0009 ProblemDetail urn:hmis:error:stale-entity) is the governing baseline. Precedent: inc-08 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED Q4 -> CR-08-Q4 (OWNER_RELEASE_GATE, gated on ADR-0017 ratification).
decision.crNeeded: CR-07-Q3 — Pessimistic (PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) row lock on WardBed/Admission bed-claim (extends parked CR-08-Q4 to the inpatient aggregate); raise only if owner elects the deviation
decision.buildImpact: 07a (Admission + Discharge + Ward) BUILDS the baseline: rely on the inherited AuditableEntity @Version on WardBed and Admission; the admit transaction loads the target WardBed, asserts status==EMPTY, transitions EMPTY->WAITING (and the activation path WAITING->OCCUPIED on ward-bed bill payment / full-cover, per N1), and lets the concurrent loser fail with OptimisticLockException -> 409 STALE_ENTITY through the global ProblemDetail handler. 07a must NOT add @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)/SELECT FOR UPDATE on WardBed, AdmissionBed, or the ward PatientBill; must NOT add a pessimistic lockBed()/lockAdmission() repository finder; must NOT introduce a new ErrorCode for bed contention (reuse STALE_ENTITY — do not invent BED_TAKEN/STOCK_CONTENDED equivalents). 07a SHOULD mirror inc-08's documentation discipline: a Javadoc/AC note on the WardBed claim path stating 'NO pessimistic lock — bed-claim race guarded by @Version/409 STALE_ENTITY; PESSIMISTIC_WRITE target parked under CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017'. Testcontainers concurrency test asserts the two-parallel-admit-to-one-EMPTY-bed case yields exactly one WAITING bed + one 409 STALE_ENTITY (parity-neutral correctness test, not a legacy-output assertion). 07b/07c: no impact — nursing-chart and consumable/ward-accrual paths are billing/chart writes guarded by their own @Version; no bed-claim contention.
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement lead only: (1) decide whether to lift the lock for the bed/admission aggregate — i.e. approve CR-07-Q3 (the deviation) AND ratify ADR-0017 (both required; ratification of ADR-0017 alone does NOT authorize a bed-claim lock because ADR-0017's pessimistic scope is stock/documents, so the bed extension still needs an explicit CR scoping decision). Until both land, the @Version-only baseline is frozen and 07a proceeds on it. No security-architect sign-off needed (this is concurrency, not RBAC). data-architect is consulted only if the lift is approved (FOR UPDATE lock ordering / deadlock-timeout mapping on WardBed).

challenge.reason: The CORE locking decision is sound and survives all three challenge axes: (Axis 1) ADR-0017 is verified STATUS PROPOSED (0017-concurrency-and-locking.md:2) and its §2 scopes PESSIMISTIC_WRITE to stock_balance/stock_batch decrement + document numbers ONLY — it never mentions WardBed/admission occupancy, so the resolution correctly refuses to claim ADR_PRE_DECIDED, correctly classifies as OWNER_RELEASE_GATE, and correctly notes a bed-claim lock is an EXTENSION to a new aggregate even post-ratification. The inc-08 precedent (CR-08-Q4 / 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED Q4, OWNER_RELEASE_GATE gated on ADR-0017 ratification) is cited verbatim and accurately. (Axis 2 — lock) No pessimistic lock is smuggled: the as-built has @Version-only (AuditableEntity.java:77-79) with the lock parked as Javadoc in StockService.java:37, StockBatchRepository.java:23, PharmacyMedicineRepository.java:13 — the resolution correctly parks the bed lock under CR-07-Q3 and tells 07a to build the @Version baseline. (Axis 3) Owner action (approve CR-07-Q3 AND ratify ADR-0017, both required; ADR-0017 alone does not authorize a bed extension) is correctly flagged, not pre-decided. WardBed cycle EMPTY->WAITING->OCCUPIED->EMPTY is legacy-verified (01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md:46-47). HOWEVER the resolution MISREPRESENTS the as-built baseline it claims to "inherit": it states the bed-claim loser is "surfaced as 409 STALE_ENTITY via the ProblemDetail handler (urn:hmis:error:stale-entity)" as existing exact-process baseline. It is NOT. (1) STALE_ENTITY does not exist in ErrorCode.java — the only inc-08 addition was INSUFFICIENT_STOCK (ErrorCode.java:120), and the inc-08 build spec (05-INC08A-BUILD-SPEC.md:26,115,266,268) explicitly froze "NO STALE_ENTITY in the 08a baseline — gated on Q4/ADR-0017." (2) GlobalExceptionHandler.java has NO @ExceptionHandler for OptimisticLockException/ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException (grep: zero matches anywhere in Java); an optimistic-lock failure today falls through the catch-all @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) at line 107-112 and surfaces as 500 INTERNAL, not 409. So the resolution's claimed "inheritance" is partly phantom: 07a building against it verbatim would either ship a 500 on the bed-claim race, or silently introduce the STALE_ENTITY ErrorCode + a new OptimisticLock handler as net-new shared-kernel work — and STALE_ENTITY surfacing is itself a parked item behind the SAME Q4/ADR-0017 gate that deferred it in inc-08. The build-impact concurrency test ("exactly one WAITING bed + one 409 STALE_ENTITY") CANNOT pass against the as-built handler. This is a baseline-accuracy defect, not a locking-posture error.
challenge.correction: Keep the locking posture verbatim: 07a builds @Version-only (no @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE), no SELECT FOR UPDATE, no lockBed()/lockAdmission() finder, no BED_TAKEN/STOCK_CONTENDED ErrorCode), with the pessimistic bed-claim lock parked as CR-07-Q3 gated on ADR-0017 ratification + explicit owner deviation approval; classification stays OWNER_RELEASE_GATE; owner action (approve CR-07-Q3 AND ratify ADR-0017) stays as flagged. CORRECT the baseline claim: stop asserting that "409 STALE_ENTITY via the ProblemDetail handler" is the inherited exact-process baseline — it is NOT as-built. As verified: (a) ErrorCode.java contains no STALE_ENTITY (only INSUFFICIENT_STOCK from inc-08); (b) GlobalExceptionHandler.java has no OptimisticLockException/ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException handler, so a stale write currently yields 500 INTERNAL, not 409; (c) inc-08 (05-INC08A-BUILD-SPEC.md:26,115,266,268) explicitly froze STALE_ENTITY OUT of the baseline as parked behind Q4/ADR-0017. Therefore the bed-claim race is, in the AS-BUILT baseline today, guarded by the @Version optimistic lock at the persistence layer (the FIRST commit wins EMPTY->WAITING; the second commit's OptimisticLockException prevents the double-claim — correctness is intact at the DB level) but is NOT yet surfaced as a clean 409 to the client. Two things follow that 07a must NOT silently absorb: (1) Adding ErrorCode.STALE_ENTITY (urn:hmis:error:stale-entity, 409) + an @ExceptionHandler(ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException.class) in GlobalExceptionHandler is NET-NEW shared-kernel work that is itself gated behind the same parked CR-08-Q4/ADR-0017 surfacing item — it must be raised as the explicit dependency for CR-07-Q3 (or a sibling shared-error CR), NOT built into the 07a baseline by assumption. (2) Re-scope the build-impact Testcontainers concurrency assertion: until the STALE_ENTITY surfacing is approved, assert only the parity-neutral CORRECTNESS invariant that survives today — two parallel admits to one EMPTY bed yield exactly ONE WAITING bed and the loser's transaction does NOT commit (i.e., no double-claim; OptimisticLockException is raised), NOT that the loser receives a 409 STALE_ENTITY (that response code is not produced by the current handler and asserting it would fail or force un-CR'd shared-kernel changes). Add the Javadoc/AC discipline note as proposed, but reword it to match reality: "NO pessimistic lock — bed-claim double-claim prevented by AuditableEntity @Version optimistic lock at commit (first commit wins EMPTY->WAITING); PESSIMISTIC_WRITE target AND clean 409 STALE_ENTITY surfacing both parked under CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017 (STALE_ENTITY is absent from the as-built ErrorCode set per inc-08 05-INC08A-BUILD-SPEC.md)." Net effect: locking decision UPHELD; the resolution must be revised to (i) stop presenting 409 STALE_ENTITY as inherited baseline, (ii) flag STALE_ENTITY surfacing as a parked net-new dependency rather than building it, and (iii) downgrade the concurrency test assertion to the no-double-claim correctness invariant.

decision.decision: 07a reproduces the legacy single-actor disposition approval EXACTLY: the discharge/referral/deceased approve path sets approvedBy = createdBy (verified PatientResource.java:5379, 5628/5679, 5905/5924) with NO approvedBy != createdBy validation. DischargePlan (and ReferralPlan, DeceasedNote) carry approvedBy/approvedOn/approvedAt fields populated by copy at finalize-from-STOPPED, and the disposition status set is PENDING -> APPROVED only. No SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN error code, no DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE privilege, no second-actor check is built in the exact-process baseline. The second-approver Segregation-of-Duties gate is PARKED as a single net-new change request, CR-07-SoD, to be approved (or rejected) by the engagement-lead before any of 07a/07b/07c builds it. CR-07-SoD bundles three coupled items the owner must rule on together: (1) approve the SoD control at all (it has zero legacy basis); (2) the IAM operation-vocabulary decision — the legacy purge loop (MainApplication.java:306-339) deletes any privilege whose suffix-after-last-dash is not in the fixed Operation set (ACCESS/ALL/CREATE/READ/UPDATE/DELETE/CANCEL/REJECT/VERIFY/APPROVE/ARCHIVE/ALLOW, per security/Operation.java), so *-DISCHARGE and *-WRITE forms are purge-incompatible and MUST NOT be specified; only an APPROVE-suffixed object split (e.g. DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE, suffix APPROVE survives) is vocabulary-legal, and choosing the object/operation split is the owner's IAM call; (3) whether to add the new ErrorCode SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN. I recommend the SoD control on its clinical-governance merits but I do NOT decide it, and I explicitly do NOT pre-author the privilege code or vocabulary extension.
decision.rationale: Verified legacy ground truth makes the second-approver gate a pure net-new control, not a missing fix: the approver is unconditionally copied from the creator in every disposition path (PatientResource.java:5379 discharge, 5628/5679 referral, 5905/5924 deceased), there is no approvedBy != createdBy comparison anywhere, the disposition endpoints are ungated (@PreAuthorize commented at :5393, only ADMISSION-CREATE is legacy-derivable), and SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN / DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE exist only in inc-07 planning docs, never in legacy or HMSCLEAN2 source (ErrorCode.java has no such code). Under the charter, an attractive improvement with no legacy process basis is reproduce-legacy + a written approved CR before implementation; it is NOT silently folded into the exact-process baseline. This is not ADR_PRE_DECIDED (no ADR addresses disposition SoD, and all ADRs are Proposed regardless). It is not merely ARCHITECTURE_DECISION because adding a SoD gate changes the business process (a second human actor must now act). The owner-only facets (approve the deviation, settle IAM vocabulary, authorize a new ErrorCode) are carried INSIDE the CR for engagement-lead sign-off rather than split into a separate gate, because they have no meaning unless the deviation itself is approved.
decision.adrCitation: none — ADRs silent on disposition approval / SoD. ADR-0007 (AuditRecorder, SHA-256 chained, Proposed) already provides tamper-evident who/when capture for approve actions, so the audit trail does NOT depend on this CR; no IAM/RBAC ADR governs the privilege vocabulary (the binding constraint is the legacy purge loop in MainApplication.java:306-339 against security/Operation.java, not an ADR).
decision.crNeeded: CR-07-SoD: Net-new second-approver Segregation-of-Duties gate on discharge/disposition approval (approvedBy != createdBy) — requires owner-approved DEVIATION, an IAM operation-vocabulary decision (APPROVE-suffixed privilege only; *-DISCHARGE/*-WRITE are purge-incompatible), and a new SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN ErrorCode.
decision.buildImpact: 07a (Admission+Discharge+Ward) BUILDS the legacy-faithful single-actor approval: finalize-from-STOPPED copies approvedBy=createdBy, approvedOn=createdOn, approvedAt=server timestamp; disposition status enum constrained to PENDING/APPROVED; the three separate disposition entities (DischargePlan/ReferralPlan/DeceasedNote) each retain approvedBy/On/At fields populated by copy. 07a MUST NOT build: any approvedBy != createdBy check, any SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN ErrorCode, any DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE (or *-DISCHARGE/*-WRITE) privilege, or any second-actor/approver-distinct worklist filter. challenge.correction: None required. The resolution is upheld as written. One optional clarity note for the CR drafter (non-blocking, does not change the verdict): when CR-07-SoD is ruled on, the legacy DECEASED disposition uses PatientType.DECEASED (Patient.setType("DECEASED"), verified at PatientResource.java:5901/5920) as the sole deceased marker — so any future SoD follow-up slice that touches the deceased path must reuse the existing PatientDeceasedEvent/PatientClosureListener pattern and must NOT introduce a boolean Patient.deceased flag, an admit-time deceased guard, or a PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode, none of which exist in legacy. decision.decision: Inc-07 reproduces the legacy baseline EXACTLY and does NOT build an admit-time deceased guard. Concretely: (a) 07a's doAdmission reproduces the legacy open-admission re-admit block verbatim — hard-reject if the patient already has any Admission in PENDING or IN-PROCESS, throwing InvalidPatientOperationException with the verbatim message "Could not process admission. The patient is already admitted" (legacy PatientResource.java:5194-5200), mapped to the existing BUSINESS_RULE (422) ErrorCode, NOT a new code. (b) The legacy admit-time field checks are reproduced exactly and NO others added: patient-exists, no-open-admission, bed-exists/active/EMPTY (PatientResource.java:5189-5205, PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1709) — there is NO patient.type=='DECEASED' branch, so 07a must NOT add one. (c) The deceased TERMINAL on the inpatient path sets the deceased marker by reusing the EXISTING PatientDeceasedEvent + PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased seam (publish PatientDeceasedEvent on inpatient deceased-note approval; the registration-side BEFORE_COMMIT listener flips PatientType=DECEASED atomically). The listener is patient-uid-keyed and source-agnostic, so the admission deceased path requires NO change to the listener — only that 07a's admission deceased-note approval publishes the same event (today ClosureService.approveDeceased throws "Admission closure path is not yet supported (deferred)" for a null-consultation note; 07a removes that deferral and wires the admission branch to publish the event). (d) The explicit admit-time deceased-block + the PATIENT_DECEASED RFC 7807 type are NET-NEW with no legacy basis and are PARKED as CR-07-deceased-guard; 07a must NOT add a PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode enum constant, must NOT add a deceased branch to the admit guard, and must NOT introduce a Patient.deceased boolean (CR-05 — DECEASED stays a PatientType value). Note: the existing open-admission block + the INPATIENT consultation block ("This operation is not allowed for inpatients") already provide partial coverage — a freshly-deceased patient whose admission has just signed-out is no longer PENDING/IN-PROCESS, so a literal re-admit IS possible in legacy; that residual gap is exactly what CR-07-deceased-guard would close, and is flagged for the owner as a clinical-safety consideration, not silently built.
decision.rationale: Verified against as-built and legacy source, not the planning doc. (1) PHANTOM confirmed: ErrorCode.java (read in full) has no PATIENT_DECEASED/ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN — only INSUFFICIENT_STOCK among inc-07 needs. (2) No legacy admit-time deceased check: discovery raw line 148 — "PatientResource.java:5189-5205 doAdmission only checks (a) patient exists, (b) no existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission, (c) bed exists... No check of patient.type=='DECEASED'. CONFIRMED ABSENT." (3) No deceased boolean: PatientType.DECEASED is the sole marker (PatientType.java:35-40, CR-05). (4) The reproduce-legacy facts ARE real: the open-admission re-admit block (raw line 48, PatientResource.java:5194-5200) and the INPATIENT consultation block (PatientResource.java:499-500, already reproduced in PatientRegistrationProcess.java:317-320). (5) The DECEASED-via-event seam already exists and is exercised on the OPD path (PatientDeceasedEvent, PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased flipping type=DECEASED BEFORE_COMMIT, published by ClosureService.approveDeceased) — the admission deceased path is a deferred extension of this same seam, NOT new infrastructure. Per the charter: an admit-time deceased guard is a tempting clinical-safety improvement with ZERO legacy basis — it would REJECT an admission the legacy accepts (a real observable process change), so it is a DEVIATION requiring an APPROVED CR before implementation, never silently folded into the exact-process baseline. It is not ADR-gated (no ADR addresses it), and because it changes admission outcomes it is not a no-process-impact ARCHITECTURE_DECISION nor a pure OWNER_RELEASE_GATE; it is a build-or-not deviation gated on a CR. From a clinical-safety standpoint, blocking the admission of a patient already recorded deceased is sound practice and worth raising — but raising it as a CR is the correct charter mechanism, not building it now.
decision.adrCitation: none — ADRs silent. No ratified or proposed ADR governs an admit-time deceased guard or the PATIENT_DECEASED error type. Governing precedents are charter rules + CR-05 (DECEASED is a PatientType value, no boolean) and the inc-05 C12 event-seam ADR (PatientDeceasedEvent/PatientClosureListener BEFORE_COMMIT atomicity). challenge.reason:
Verified against as-built code and the sourced legacy discovery extract (file:line citations to D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/...), not the planning doc. All three challenge axes pass.

AXIS 1 (ADR): No mis-citation, no inc-08-style trap. The resolution explicitly states "none — ADRs silent" and does NOT claim ADR_PRE_DECIDED for anything (so the ADR-0017/0018 "Proposed-but-treated-as-binding" re-classification rule does not fire). Its grounding is correct: (a) CR-05 — DECEASED is a PatientType value with NO boolean — confirmed at registration/domain/PatientType.java:35-40 ("type='DECEASED' is the sole 'deceased' marker — no boolean flag (CR-05)"); (b) the inc-05 C12 event seam (PatientDeceasedEvent in shared.event + PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased @TransactionalEventListener BEFORE_COMMIT flipping type=DECEASED) is real and the listener is patient-uid-keyed + source-agnostic (PatientClosureListener.java:84-102, PatientDeceasedEvent.java:29), so the admission path needs NO listener change.

AXIS 2 (no smuggled deviation): The resolution does the OPPOSITE of smuggling. The inc-07 planning doc itself is the smuggle vector — docs/delivery/increments/07-inpatient-nursing.md:64,82,91,116 mandates building a Patient.deceased boolean, a PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode, an admit()/book() guard, and even labels "deceased guard" a "behavioural golden-master PARITY test" at line 116. The resolution correctly REFUSES all of that as a phantom/deviation, parks it as CR-07-deceased-guard, and explicitly forbids 07a from adding the ErrorCode constant, the type==DECEASED admit branch, or the boolean. Confirmed phantom: ErrorCode.java (read in full) has only INSUFFICIENT_STOCK among inc-07 needs — no PATIENT_DECEASED. Confirmed legacy absence: discovery raw line 147-148 — "PatientResource.java:5189-5205 doAdmission only checks (a) patient exists, (b) no existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission, (c) bed exists... No check of patient.type=='DECEASED'. CONFIRMED ABSENT." The plan-claim "admit()/book() reject the patient (PATIENT_DECEASED)" is verdict PHANTOM (discovery line 192-195) / DRIFT (line 344-348). What 07a IS told to build is pure verified legacy baseline: the open-admission re-admit hard-reject with the verbatim message and BUSINESS_RULE 422 (discovery line 48-49, PatientResource.java:5194-5200), the exact legacy admit field-checks with no deceased branch, and wiring the admission deceased-note approval to publish the EXISTING event (removing the "Admission closure path is not yet supported (deferred)" throw confirmed at ClosureService.java:210-214). No new ServiceKind, no second-approver gate, no pessimistic lock, no consumables-from-stock, no WardTransfer, no boolean.

AXIS 3 (classification + owner flag): DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR is correct — an admit-time deceased guard would reject an admission legacy accepts (observable process change), so it is a CR-gated build, not a no-process-impact architecture decision nor a pure owner-release-gate. The residual clinical-safety gap (a signed-out, now-DECEASED patient is no longer PENDING/IN-PROCESS, so legacy permits a literal re-admit) is correctly surfaced to engagement-lead + HDE as an owner decision and NOT silently decided either way.

The legacy disposition choreography the resolution relies on is also confirmed exactly: discovery line 93 — legacy deceased-approve flips Patient.type='DECEASED' only when admission.status=='HELD', frees WardBed to EMPTY at HELD (before summary approval), and does NOT stamp dischargedAt. The as-built reproduces the legacy inline type=DECEASED via the already-ratified C12 event seam (a behaviour-preserving modernization, not net-new infra), which is the correct exact-process posture.
challenge.correction:
No correction required to the decision, classification, ADR citation, CR, or owner action — all are accurate and charter-compliant. Two non-blocking ADVISORY notes for the 07a build package and the owner, to prevent drift during implementation:

The patient is already admitted" triggered when the patient has ANY Admission in status PENDING OR IN-PROCESS (legacy free-text Admission.status, discovery line 93/965). 07a must reject on the {PENDING, IN-PROCESS} set (not a narrower single-state check) and emit that exact string, mapped to BUSINESS_RULE (422). decision.decision: CONFIRMED: DECEASED stays a PatientType enum value. There is NO boolean Patient.deceased field and 07a must not introduce one. The deceased terminal in inpatient sets Patient.type=DECEASED by REUSING the existing inc-04/05 C12 cross-module event seam exactly as the clinical OPD death-note path already does. Concrete seam: (1) inpatient's DeceasedNote-finalize application service (07a, disposition path) publishes the EXISTING record shared.event.PatientDeceasedEvent(String patientUid, String actorUsername) via ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(...) INSIDE its own @Transactional method — same in-tx publish pattern as clinical ClosureService.java:241-242. (2) registration.application.PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased(...) consumes it with @TransactionalEventListener(phase=BEFORE_COMMIT) and calls patient.changeType(PatientType.DECEASED) (Patient.java:269) on the managed entity (dirty-check flush, no save()), plus auditRecorder.record(\"registration.Patient\", uid, UPDATE, actorUsername) for SEC-01. No new event type, no new listener, no new field, no new ErrorCode are created for Q6. inpatient imports ONLY shared.event when publishing; registration imports ONLY shared.event when consuming — so NO inpatient->registration and NO registration->inpatient compile edge is added; ApplicationModules.verify() stays green. EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE of Q6: the deceased-readmit guard / PATIENT_DECEASED error code (that is Q5/D24, a separate NET-NEW CR — legacy has no admit-time deceased check) and the actual DeceasedNote disposition workflow rules (date/time split, patientSummary+causeOfDeath validation, IN-PROCESS->HELD->free-bed choreography, bills-cleared gate) which are 07a build items resolved elsewhere, not by this enum/event confirmation.
decision.rationale: The question is a placement/seam confirmation with zero process impact: it neither changes nor reproduces any business rule, workflow state, numbering, pricing, or report output — it only confirms WHERE the deceased marker lives (enum, not boolean) and HOW the type flip is wired (existing event vs a new field). That is squarely a solution-architect call, so I decide it rather than parking it. It is NOT ADR_PRE_DECIDED because, per docs/adr/README.md line 3, every ADR (including ADR-0008/0014/0022 that govern the seam) is still status Proposed/unratified — but the charter's owner-gate only fires for an ADR TARGET that needs ratification to proceed; here the seam ALREADY EXISTS as built and verified code (the inc-05 C12 path), so no unratified ADR is being relied on to authorize net-new work. It is NOT OWNER_RELEASE_GATE because there is no owner-only sign-off: the decision introduces no deviation, no new privilege, no RBAC change, and no schema change requiring owner approval (the reconciliation table tagged engagement-lead as owner, but the question text itself scopes this as an architecture confirmation with 'no legacy-process deviation'). It is NOT DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR because reusing the enum+event IS the reproduce-legacy baseline — legacy's sole deceased marker is the type, set by the deceased-note approval; a boolean field would itself be the un-asked-for divergence. Verified facts: PatientType.java:37-40 ('type=DECEASED is the sole deceased marker — no boolean flag (CR-05)'); grep of Patient.java shows no boolean deceased field; Patient.changeType() at Patient.java:269; PatientDeceasedEvent record in shared.event; PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased uses BEFORE_COMMIT with no cross-module import; clinical ClosureService.java:241-242 is the existing in-tx publisher precedent inpatient mirrors.
decision.adrCitation: ADR-0008 (Bounded-Context Decomposition / inter-module contracts) + ADR-0014 (Cross-cutting Conventions: shared is OPEN, events live in shared.event) + ADR-0022 D5 / inc-05 C12 ADR (the PatientDeceasedEvent cross-module event seam). All three are status Proposed (pending engagement-lead ratification, docs/adr/README.md line 3), so they are not cited as binding pre-decisions — but the seam they describe is already built-and-verified code (PatientClosureListener + ClosureService), so Q6 relies on existing reality, not on an unratified ADR. CR-05 governs the no-boolean-flag enum modelling.
decision.crNeeded: none (for Q6 itself). Separately note: the deceased-READMIT guard + PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode is Q5/D24 — that NET-NEW item needs its own CR ('CR-07: add PATIENT_DECEASED admit-time readmit guard') and is NOT authorized by this Q6 confirmation.
decision.buildImpact: 07a MUST: in the DeceasedNote-finalize/disposition application service, publish the EXISTING shared.event.PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername) in-tx via ApplicationEventPublisher (mirror clinical ClosureService.java:241-242); rely on the EXISTING registration PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased to flip Patient.type→DECEASED. 07a MUST NOT: (a) add a boolean Patient.deceased field or any new deceased column; (b) add a second/duplicate PatientDeceasedEvent type or a second listener — reuse shared.event.PatientDeceasedEvent verbatim; (c) call registration code or patient.changeType() directly from inpatient (no cross-module compile edge — go through the event only); (d) add a PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode or any admit-time deceased check under cover of Q6 (that is the parked Q5 CR). 07b and 07c: NO build impact from Q6 (nursing charts and consumable/accrual do not touch the deceased marker). decision.decision: Inc-07 REPRODUCES THE AS-BUILT CLINICAL PRECEDENT: the inpatient (admission-path) ReferralPlan carries the provider as a MANDATORY loose `external_medical_provider_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL` (NOT NULL + @NotBlank, updatable=false), with NO foreign key and NO existence check — byte-for-byte the same column shape and constraints already merged for the clinical/OPD ReferralPlan in V28 (and patient ref made loose by V37). Inc-07 does NOT build an `external_medical_providers` table, NOT a referral bounded-context masterdata entity, NOT seed data, and NOT a real FK. The legacy mandatory-provider business rule (a referral MUST name a provider) is preserved via NOT NULL + @NotBlank — the constraint is kept; only its physical enforcement mechanism (FK vs loose uid) follows the established HMSCLEAN2 cross-module-reference convention. Building the ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata + real FK is parked as a CR (option (a)), not built in 07a/07b/07c.
decision.rationale: Two competing legacy-faithful facts. (1) The legacy DOES enforce a hard FK: ReferralPlan.java:49-52 declares @ManyToOne(targetEntity=ExternalMedicalProvider.class, optional=false) + @JoinColumn(nullable=false), backed by a real ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata entity (code/name/address/telephone/email/fax/website/active) with full Resource+Repository+Service. So the DISCH-5 'legacy was free-text' premise is PROVABLY WRONG — flagged. (2) BUT the inc-05 as-built already RESOLVED how HMSCLEAN2 models this exact column: clinical V28 + ReferralPlan.java:110-124 chose a MANDATORY loose uid with NO FK because the referral bounded context / external_medical_providers table is deliberately not built yet; V37 then made even the patient ref loose (ADR-0022 D2 cross-module convention). That decision is merged and is the standing precedent. The business PROCESS the charter protects (a referral must name a provider, mandatory) is identical under either mechanism — NOT NULL + @NotBlank reproduces the legacy mandatory rule exactly. The FK-vs-loose-uid choice is a data-model/enforcement detail, and the engagement has already settled it the loose-uid way. Reproducing the as-built precedent is therefore the charter-default baseline (consistency + no net-new masterdata surface); switching inc-07 to a real FK would silently diverge from the merged clinical model AND require a whole net-new masterdata table/seed/CRUD — that is the deviation needing a CR, NOT the loose uid. Classified EXACT_PROCESS_REPRODUCE: inc-07 faithfully reproduces both the legacy mandatory-provider rule and the established HMSCLEAN2 physical modelling of it.
decision.adrCitation: none — ADRs silent on referral provider masterdata. ADR-0019 mentions ExternalMedicalProvider only as a 'stored email never sent' example, not the FK question. The governing precedent is the as-built migration V28 (clinical referral_plans) + V37 (loose patient_uid) + the ADR-0022 D2 cross-module-loose-ref convention they follow — engineering precedent, not a ratified ADR.

(Park; not in 07a/07b/07c.)

decision.decision: CONFIRM WardTransfer is NET-NEW and EXCLUDE it from inc-07 exact-process scope. The reproduce-legacy baseline is "legacy has no ward-to-ward / bed-to-bed transfer," so the exact-process baseline builds NO transfer: no WardTransfer entity, no POST /transfer-ward (or any reassign-bed endpoint), no TRANSFERRED admission status, and NO exact-process acceptance criteria of any kind for transfer. AdmissionStatus enum is fixed at PENDING / IN-PROCESS / STOPPED / HELD / SIGNED-OUT (1:1 with legacy strings) — TRANSFERRED must NOT be added. Admission carries a single wardBed relation set ONCE at admit and never re-assigned mid-stay; AdmissionBed ledger likewise has no transfer/close-and-reopen-new-bed path. Separately, I am parking a candidate change request, CR-07-ward-transfer, in the change-request register so the capability is captured rather than lost. The baseline exclusion proceeds now with no owner action; the CR stays UNAPPROVED and unscheduled until engagement-lead decides at a phase gate whether to fund it (later increment vs defer entirely). If/when approved, its acceptance criteria MUST be written as net-new requirements (new ward-bed state choreography, billing/insurance re-anchoring on transfer, audit trail) and MUST NOT be framed as reproducing legacy behaviour, since there is no legacy behaviour to reproduce.
decision.rationale: Verified against legacy source, not just the doc: (1) the ONLY *Transfer artefacts in the entire legacy codebase are ConsultationTransfer.java + its repository, and ConsultationTransfer is an OUTPATIENT clinic-to-clinic transfer (Patient+Consultation+Clinic FKs) wholly unrelated to ward beds — there is no WardTransfer/BedTransfer class. (2) Grep for transfer-ward|TRANSFERRED|transferWard|WardTransfer across all legacy Java returns ZERO functional matches (only CKEditor/license/IDE noise). (3) Every setWardBed/setWard call site is admit-time bed assignment (PatientServiceImpl:1718, :1779; UpdatePatient:330) or ward master-data CRUD (WardServiceImpl:141) or a read-only report projection (ReportResource) — there is NO code path that re-assigns a patient's bed or ward after admission. Doc-correction worth recording: the reconciliation headline says 'both bed FKs updatable=false,' but Admission.wardBed at Admission.java:63 is actually updatable=true; this does NOT change the verdict — updatable=true merely means the column COULD persist on update, yet no business logic ever mutates wardBed on an in-process admission, so the transfer capability is structurally absent regardless of the column attribute. Per charter, a tempting improvement (mid-stay ward/bed transfer is a genuine hospital workflow and clinical-safety nicety) with no legacy basis is NOT silently absorbed: the exact-process baseline is reproduce-legacy (= no transfer), and any deliberate deviation requires a written, approved CR BEFORE implementation. Hence DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR, not a phantom to merely delete and forget: I excise the phantom from the baseline (a scope decision I own as BA) AND register the deviation so it can be governed. It is not ARCHITECTURE_DECISION (transfer is a process change with billing/insurance/state-machine impact, not a placement question), not ADR_PRE_DECIDED (no ADR addresses ward transfer), and not OWNER_RELEASE_GATE for the EXCLUSION itself (the question states no owner action is needed to proceed with the baseline exclusion — reproduce-legacy is the default the BA applies); the owner gate applies only to APPROVING the CR if the feature is later wanted.

decision.decision: Inc-07 (07a ward billing) REPRODUCES the active-flag-ignored quirk VERBATIM in the exact-process baseline. Insurance ward-price eligibility selects from covered rows only — keyed on (insurancePlan, covered=true) — and the max-price-OR-exact-plan-match selection loop must check ONLY price and plan-id identity. The WardTypeInsurancePlan.active flag is carried as a persisted/CRUD-editable column (so masterdata round-trips losslessly) but MUST NOT participate in the eligibility query or selection loop. An inactive-but-covered plan therefore stays eligible, exactly as legacy. 07a MUST also build the two confirmed-accurate companion behaviours that travel with this loop and are currently a deferred billing-engine gap: (1) the max-price-or-exact-plan-match selection (highest covered price wins unless an exact patient-plan-id match is found, which short-circuits) producing the COVERED principal ward bill at eligiblePlan.price; and (2) the supplementary TOP-UP bill — when eligiblePlan is a different plan than the patient's own AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0, create a second PatientBill status=UNPAID, billItem=Bed, description \"Ward Bed / Room (Top up)\", linked principal<->supplementary, with its own cash PatientInvoice/PatientInvoiceDetail (qty=1). The top-up split is LOAD-BEARING for the admission activation gate (verified PatientServiceImpl.java:1950-1963): only the no-top-up branch (exact-plan match or full coverage) flips admission to IN-PROCESS + bed to OCCUPIED at admit; the top-up branch leaves admission PENDING / bed WAITING, deferring activation to top-up-bill payment. The top-up split must be BUILT in 07a, not consciously dropped — dropping it would silently break both ward-claim parity and the N1 activation gate. CR-fixing the active-flag (i.e. adding active=true to eligibility so inactive plans become ineligible) is the DEVIATION path and is NOT built into the baseline absent an approved CR.
decision.rationale: Verified against legacy source, not the doc. WardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.java:40 exposes only findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, boolean); there is no active-aware query. The selection loop at PatientServiceImpl.java:1799-1809 branches solely on plan.getPrice() > eligiblePrice OR plan.getInsurancePlan().getId() == patient.getInsurancePlan().getId() — isActive() is never read. WardTypeInsurancePlan.java:43 carries `private boolean active = false` but it is dead weight in eligibility. So the quirk is real and reproducible. Charter default is reproduce-verbatim; honouring the active flag is an operationally sensible improvement (an admin who deactivates a ward-insurance price row would reasonably expect it to stop being selectable, and stale/inactive coverage tiers leaking into NHIF ward claims is a genuine claims-integrity risk) — but it has NO legacy basis, so it is a deliberate deviation that requires an APPROVED CR before implementation, not a silent baseline build. The clinical/claims risk is noted and routed to the owner via the CR, satisfying the surface-the-conflict guardrail without overriding exact-process. The top-up split + selection loop, by contrast, are NOT deviations: they are confirmed-accurate legacy behaviour (synthesis newDrift N2/N3, raw cites PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1949) that the current PriceLookupImpl explicitly defers to the billing increment (PriceLookupImpl.java:33-38) — so they are mandatory exact-process build items for 07a, and their absence must NOT be quietly carried because the top-up branch gates admission activation.
decision.adrCitation: none — ADRs silent on ward insurance eligibility semantics. No ratified or proposed ADR governs WardTypeInsurancePlan active-flag handling or the ward top-up split. (ADR-0018 concerns ward-DAY accrual scheduling, not admit-time eligibility/pricing; it does not touch this.) Because no ADR target exists, this is not OWNER_RELEASE_GATE-by-ADR — it is a clean charter-default reproduce + a deviation CR for the fix.
decision.crNeeded: CR-07-Q9: Honour WardTypeInsurancePlan.active in insurance ward-price eligibility (exclude inactive-but-covered plans from the selection loop) — claims-integrity hardening, deviation from legacy active-flag-ignored behaviour
decision.buildImpact: 07a MUST BUILD: (a) the WardType.price cash anchor + the WardTypeInsurancePlan-equivalent covered-row source for INSURANCE; (b) the max-price-OR-exact-plan-match selection loop checking ONLY price and plan-id (NO active check); (c) the COVERED principal ward bill at eligiblePlan.price + its insurance PatientInvoice/Detail (qty=1); (d) the supplementary TOP-UP bill (UNPAID, \"Ward Bed / Room (Top up)\", principal<->supplementary linkage, own cash invoice/detail qty=1) when eligiblePlan differs from patient plan AND difference>0; (e) the activation-gate wiring where the top-up branch leaves PENDING/WAITING and the no-top-up branch flips IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED. 07a MUST NOT BUILD: any active-flag filter in the eligibility query or selection loop (that is the CR-07-Q9 deviation, baseline reproduces the quirk). PriceLookupImpl stays a pure row-lookup primitive — the selection-loop + top-up billing logic belongs in 07a ward-billing application code, NOT in masterdata PriceLookupImpl (per its own DO-NOT-add-billing-logic contract, PriceLookupImpl.java:33-38). 07b/07c: no direct impact, but 07c ward-day accrual must re-accrue against the SAME principal ward price source so per-24h totals reconcile, and must not silently introduce an active-flag filter that 07a deliberately omitted. QA: add an exact-process scenario proving an inactive-but-covered WardTypeInsurancePlan row is still selected (the quirk), plus a top-up-split golden-master (different plan, difference>0 → UNPAID supplementary bill + admission stays PENDING until top-up paid).
decision.ownerActionRequired: Engagement-lead must rule on CR-07-Q9 (the active-flag fix). HDE recommendation: APPROVE the CR as low-risk claims-integrity hardening because an inactive coverage tier leaking into NHIF ward claims is a real submission-correctness/audit risk — but this is the owner's call, not the HDE's, and absent approval the baseline reproduces the quirk. Therefore: do NOT build a CONSUMABLE_ISSUE decrement seam; do NOT promote StockService/StoreStockService.decrementFefo to @NamedInterface for inpatient; do NOT reuse PharmacyStockCredit.debitTransferOut (it writes a transfer-flavoured TRANSFER_OUT card, semantically wrong). The 'consumables draw from stock' behaviour is a NET-NEW improvement with no legacy basis: it is captured as a DEVIATION CR (CR-07-consumable-stock) for the owner to fund or decline — it is NOT silently built into the baseline. Consequence: the D5 'last-unit 409' concurrency test is REMOVED as an exact-process AC (there is no stock contention on a billing-only path) and may only exist as a CR-scoped test if CR-07-consumable-stock is approved.
decision.rationale: Charter rule: business PROCESS stays identical to legacy; a deviation requires a written APPROVED CR BEFORE implementation and is never silently folded into the exact-process baseline. Legacy consumable issue is conclusively billing-only — PatientServiceImpl.savePatientConsumableChart (verified 2250-2475) builds a PatientBill (+ optional PatientInvoiceDetail) and saves the chart, with no stock entity, lot, or ledger interaction anywhere in the method. Making inpatient consumable issue decrement inc-08 stock is an inventory-accuracy improvement with NO legacy basis (the doc's named dependency ConsumableStockBalance.decrementForIssue is a confirmed phantom — zero source matches; it lives only in docs). This is precisely the inc-06/inc-08 trap: a tempting idealized feature must be reproduced-as-legacy + parked as a CR, not built. decision.decision: Charge inpatient consumables under the EXISTING ServiceKind.MEDICINE. Do NOT add ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE. PriceLookup.resolve(planUid, MEDICINE, medicineUid, ccy) reproduces the legacy path exactly: legacy cash price = chart.getMedicine().getPrice() (PatientServiceImpl.java:2308,2310) and legacy insurance price = MedicineInsurancePlan via findByMedicineAndInsurancePlanAndCovered(med, plan, true) (PatientServiceImpl.java:2323,2326-2327) — which is precisely what ServiceKind.MEDICINE keys to (medicine_insurance_plans by Medicine.uid; ServiceKind.java:37, PriceLookup.java:30-37). The ChargeRequest carries kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=Medicine.uid, qty=chart.qty, inpatient=true; SettlementPolicy.requiresPrepayment returns false (settle at discharge), giving the legacy 3-way outcome (UNPAID cash | COVERED via MedicineInsurancePlan + PatientInvoiceDetail | VERIFIED cash-on-admission). To preserve the legacy display identity, set ChargeRequest.billItem='Medication' (legacy literal, PatientServiceImpl.java:2312) and description='Consumable: <medicineName>' — the consumable identity lives in the description + the separate PatientConsumableChart row, NOT in the pricing kind. No schema change, no CHECK migration, no CR.
decision.rationale: The MEDICINE-vs-CONSUMABLE choice has ZERO observable business-process or output impact: both would resolve the identical price off the identical Medicine.price / MedicineInsurancePlan data, produce the identical bill-status trichotomy, the identical PatientInvoiceDetail line (qty=1 quirk N14 preserved separately), and the identical bill description. It is purely an internal model-placement question (which enum value carries the charge), fully within Solution-Architect authority and requiring no owner sign-off and no legacy-behaviour assertion that needs legacy-analyst confirmation. The legacy itself does NOT model consumables as a distinct pricing kind — its bill line is literally billItem='Medication' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2312) priced via the medicine pricing tables. ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE is therefore a tempting normalization improvement with NO legacy basis; introducing it would convert this into a DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR (new pricing kind + CHECK migration on both service_prices ck_service_prices_kind V9:110-111 AND patient_bills ck_patient_bills_kind V15:73-75) for zero behavioural benefit. The charter ('modern design, exact process'; deviate only with an approved CR) mandates the no-CR, no-schema-change, legacy-faithful placement: MEDICINE. No ADR pre-decides this (all ADRs are Proposed; none addresses consumable pricing-kind placement), so the no-re-litigate rule does not fire and there is no owner ratification gate to wait on.

(2) The internal MEDICINE label is 'Medicine' (BillingChargeService.labelFor, line 276) with description=billItem (line 116) -- but legacy writes billItem='Medication' and description='Consumable: <name>' (verified PatientServiceImpl.java:2312-2313). Reusing recordClinicalCharge unchanged emits 'Medicine'/'Medicine', a verified display-identity DRIFT from the legacy bill-line literals; achieving the legacy strings requires extending the billing API (a real change, NOT the claimed 'no schema change, zero build impact'). (3) Legacy cash-UNPAID consumable sets bill amount=unit price (Medicine.getPrice() at :2308, no x qty) with balance=price x qty (:2310), whereas as-built computeAmount(MEDICINE) returns price x qty for the amount (BillingChargeService.java:260-265); Q13's 'identical PatientBill line' claim does not address this and it must be golden-master reconciled, not asserted. ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED WITH CORRECTION APPLIED (challenge REVISE — locking posture UPHELD, baseline claim CORRECTED). LOCKING POSTURE (unchanged): 07a builds @Version-only on WardBed/Admission (no @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE), no SELECT FOR UPDATE, no lockBed()/lockAdmission() finder, no BED_TAKEN/STOCK_CONTENDED ErrorCode); the pessimistic bed-claim lock is PARKED as CR-07-Q3 gated on ADR-0017 ratification + explicit owner deviation approval (ADR-0017 §2 scopes pessimistic locking to stock_balance/stock_batch + document numbers, so a bed-claim lock is an EXTENSION even post-ratification). Inherits inc-08's CR-08-Q4 posture. CORRECTION APPLIED (verified at source): STOP asserting '409 STALE_ENTITY via the ProblemDetail handler' is the inherited as-built baseline — it is NOT. ErrorCode.java contains NO STALE_ENTITY (only INSUFFICIENT_STOCK); GlobalExceptionHandler has NO OptimisticLockException/ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException handler (verified zero matches across the backend), so a stale write currently surfaces as 500 INTERNAL, not 409; inc-08 (05-INC08A-BUILD-SPEC.md) explicitly froze STALE_ENTITY OUT of the baseline as parked behind Q4/ADR-0017. CORRECTED BASELINE: the bed-claim double-claim is prevented today by the @Version optimistic lock at commit (first commit wins EMPTY->WAITING; the loser's OptimisticLockException prevents the double-claim — correctness intact at the DB level) but is NOT yet surfaced as a clean 409. CONSEQUENCES 07a MUST NOT silently absorb: (a) adding ErrorCode.STALE_ENTITY (urn:hmis:error:stale-entity, 409) + an @ExceptionHandler(ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException.class) is NET-NEW shared-kernel work gated behind the SAME parked CR-08-Q4/ADR-0017 surfacing item — raise it as an explicit dependency of CR-07-Q3 (or a sibling shared-error CR), do NOT build it into the 07a baseline by assumption; (b) the Testcontainers concurrency test asserts ONLY the parity-neutral correctness invariant that survives today — two parallel admits to one EMPTY bed yield exactly ONE WAITING bed and the loser's tx does NOT commit (no double-claim; OptimisticLockException raised) — NOT that the loser receives a 409 STALE_ENTITY. Javadoc/AC discipline note reworded to: 'NO pessimistic lock — bed-claim double-claim prevented by AuditableEntity @Version optimistic lock at commit (first commit wins EMPTY->WAITING); PESSIMISTIC_WRITE target AND clean 409 STALE_ENTITY surfacing both parked under CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017 (STALE_ENTITY absent from the as-built ErrorCode set per inc-08).'

CONFIRMED: DECEASED stays a PatientType enum value; NO boolean Patient.deceased field and 07a MUST NOT introduce one (verified: Patient.java's only boolean is 'active'; PatientType.java:35-40 'sole deceased marker, no boolean flag (CR-05)').
