No new event type, listener, field, or ErrorCode for Q6. 07a MUST publish from its OWN inpatient service (NOT route through clinical.ClosureService.approveDeceased, which is OPD-only and throws 'deferred' when consultation==null); MUST NOT add a boolean field, a duplicate event/listener, a direct inpatient->registration compile edge (event-only), or a PATIENT_DECEASED ErrorCode/admit-time check under cover of Q6 (that is the parked Q5 item). Reviewer merge gate: ApplicationModules.verify() stays green with no new inpatient<->registration edge; inpatient publisher and registration listener each touch only shared.event. The DeceasedNote disposition workflow rules (date/time split, summary+causeOfDeath validation, IN-PROCESS->HELD->free-bed choreography, bills-cleared gate) are 07a build items resolved elsewhere, OUT of scope of this enum/event confirmation.

RATIFIED AS DECIDED (challenge UPHELD). Inc-07 reproduces the legacy mandatory-provider RULE via the merged as-built mechanism: the inpatient (admission-path) ReferralPlan carries the provider as a MANDATORY loose external_medical_provider_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL (@NotBlank, updatable=false), NO foreign key, NO existence check — byte-for-byte the clinical/OPD ReferralPlan shape merged in V28 (V37 made the patient ref loose per ADR-0022 D2). 07a reuses the existing clinical referral_plans table/entity and its admission_uid loose ref; does NOT build an external_medical_providers table, referral masterdata entity, seed data, or a real FK. The legacy mandatory rule (a referral MUST name a provider) is preserved via NOT NULL + @NotBlank; only the physical enforcement mechanism follows the established HMSCLEAN2 loose-uid convention. CORRECTION TO INC-07 BUILD DOC (verified): the DISCH-5 'legacy was free-text' premise is PROVABLY WRONG — legacy ReferralPlan.java:49-52 is @ManyToOne(targetEntity=ExternalMedicalProvider.class, optional=false) + @JoinColumn(nullable=false) backed by a real ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata entity with full Resource+Repository+Service; the loose-uid choice is a deliberate HMSCLEAN2 convention, not a reproduction of a non-existent legacy free-text field. Building the ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata + real FK is PARKED as CR-INC07-Q7 (scoped to BOTH clinical V28 and inpatient referral plans for consistency). ADVISORY FOLDED (label precision, non-blocking): only the legacy RULE is reproduced (via the loose-uid mechanism), NOT the legacy physical FK enforcement — a more exact label is 'reproduce-legacy-RULE via merged as-built mechanism; park masterdata as CR'; when CR-INC07-Q7 is raised, reconcile ADR-0022's 'Accepted' self-status against the README 'Proposed/pending ratification' register so the CR is not built on an unratified-ADR assumption.

RATIFIED AS DECIDED (challenge UPHELD). CONFIRM WardTransfer is NET-NEW and EXCLUDE it from inc-07 exact-process scope. The reproduce-legacy baseline is 'legacy has no ward-to-ward/bed-to-bed transfer': 07a builds NO WardTransfer entity, NO POST /transfer-ward (or any reassign-bed endpoint), NO TRANSFERRED admission status, and NO exact-process acceptance criteria for transfer. AdmissionStatus enum is FROZEN at PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT (1:1 with legacy strings); TRANSFERRED MUST NOT be added. Admission.wardBed is set ONCE at admit and is immutable in business logic; the AdmissionBed ledger has no transfer/close-and-reopen-new-bed path. Verified: the only *Transfer artefacts in legacy are ConsultationTransfer (outpatient clinic-to-clinic, Patient+Consultation+Clinic FKs, no ward bed); zero functional matches for transfer-ward|TRANSFERRED|WardTransfer; every setWardBed/setWard site is admit-time assignment (PatientServiceImpl:1718) or the AdmissionBed ledger or read-only report projection — no post-admission re-assignment exists. The capability is PARKED as UNAPPROVED CR-07-ward-transfer; if later approved its ACs MUST be authored as net-new requirements (ward-bed state choreography, billing/insurance re-anchoring, audit trail) and MUST NOT be framed as reproducing legacy behaviour. Add a NEGATIVE-scope note in the RTM so the intentional absence is not mistaken for a defect. DOC CORRECTION (verified, folded): Admission.wardBed at Admission.java:62-65 is updatable=TRUE (@JoinColumn nullable=true updatable=true), NOT updatable=false as the 01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md headline and PHANTOM bullet (line 16) state — immaterial to the verdict (no business logic ever mutates wardBed post-admission, so transfer is structurally absent) but the data-migration-engineer/legacy-analyst should patch the stale doc line so the HMSCLEAN2 Flyway DDL does not mark the FK non-updatable on the strength of it.

RATIFIED AS DECIDED (challenge UPHELD). 07a ward billing REPRODUCES the active-flag-ignored quirk VERBATIM: insurance ward-price eligibility selects from covered rows only (findByInsurancePlanAndCovered, no active-aware query) and the max-price-OR-exact-plan-match selection loop checks ONLY price and plan-id identity (PatientServiceImpl.java:1801-1809), never isActive(). The WardTypeInsurancePlan.active column is carried as persisted/CRUD-editable (lossless masterdata round-trip) but MUST NOT participate in eligibility — an inactive-but-covered plan stays eligible, exactly as legacy. 07a MUST ALSO build the two confirmed-accurate companion behaviours (currently a deferred billing-engine gap per PriceLookupImpl.java:33-38): (1) the max-price-or-exact-plan-match selection producing the COVERED principal ward bill at eligiblePlan.price; (2) the supplementary TOP-UP bill (UNPAID, billItem=Bed, description 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)', principal<->supplementary linkage, own cash PatientInvoice/Detail qty=1) when eligiblePlan differs from the patient's plan AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0. The top-up split is LOAD-BEARING for the activation gate (PatientServiceImpl.java:1950-1963): the no-top-up branch flips admission IN-PROCESS + bed OCCUPIED at admit; the top-up branch leaves admission PENDING/bed WAITING until top-up-bill payment. The top-up split MUST be built, not dropped. Honouring the active flag (excluding inactive-but-covered plans) is the DEVIATION PARKED as CR-07-Q9 (claims-integrity hardening; HDE recommends APPROVE but it is the owner's call). ADVISORIES FOLDED (non-blocking): (1) the top-up branch is effectively (near-)UNREACHABLE under the legacy query scope because findByInsurancePlanAndCovered already restricts rows to the patient's OWN plan, so the loop short-circuits on the first row and the eligiblePlan.id != patient.plan.id guard rarely/never fires; BUILD the code path verbatim regardless (reproduce-verbatim requires the path to exist exactly), and the golden-master must reproduce the SAME (near-)unreachability — an own-plan covered admission yields NO top-up and DOES flip IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED; do NOT 'fix' the query scope to make top-up reachable absent a CR. (2) 07c ward-day accrual must re-accrue against the SAME principal ward price source, must not introduce an active-flag filter 07a omits, and must reconcile against the legacy rolling-24h polling-thread total (NOT ADR-0018's incorrect single-flat-charge premise).

ratifiedDecisions.topic: AdmissionBed not closed at discharge (legacy leak)
ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED AS DECIDED (challenge UPHELD). Reproduce the legacy leak VERBATIM: 07a's AdmissionBed ledger leaves the final AdmissionBed in status OPENED (closedAt=NULL) at discharge/sign-out. AdmissionBed is closed (status='CLOSED', closedAt set) ONLY at accrual rollover in the net-new ward-accrual job (07c) on a >=24h rollover AND in the multi-OPENED cleanup branch — exactly as legacy UpdatePatient.java:276-278/:297-298. No discharge/terminal path (07a) sets AdmissionBed CLOSED or closedAt; the terminal sets only WardBed=EMPTY (deceased frees at HELD), matching the grep-confirmed absence (PatientResource.java terminal paths 5359-5370/5724-5732 touch only WardBed, never AdmissionBed; PatientServiceImpl references AdmissionBed only at admit, line 1776, create OPENED). WardBed (physical master, reset EMPTY) and AdmissionBed (occupancy/billing ledger, left OPENED) stay DISTINCT entities/tables — NOT merged. 07a builds the ledger (status OPENED/CLOSED CHECK-constrained, openedAt timestamptz NOT NULL, closedAt NULL, mandatory @OneToOne FK to the ward-bed PatientBill, FKs to admission/patient/ward_bed non-updatable). DATA-MIGRATION GUARDRAIL: the reconciliation strategy MUST EXPECT dangling OPENED AdmissionBed rows for discharged admissions (do NOT flag as orphans); 3 years of AdmissionBed rows reconcile 1:1 against their @OneToOne PatientBill; do NOT add an integrity check asserting 'every terminal admission has all AdmissionBeds CLOSED' (would fail against legacy-faithful data). The close-at-discharge fix is PARKED as CR-07-Q10 (optional cleanup; does NOT gate the baseline). ADVISORY FOLDED (07c-lane, non-blocking): the legacy multi-OPENED branch (UpdatePatient.java:272-282) closes ALL OPENED beds and continues WITHOUT opening a replacement (i<no loop guard + j=j++ no-op pattern), i.e. 'close ALL OPENED + skip-charge that cycle' — 07c's accrual golden-master must reproduce that precisely, NOT a 'close all but one' reading; zero impact on the 07a verbatim-leak baseline.

ratifiedDecisions.topic: Consumable qty=1 quirk + mislabeled credit-note ref (+ unconditional parent-invoice delete)
ratifiedDecisions.ratifiedDecision: RATIFIED WITH CORRECTION APPLIED (challenge REVISE — two named quirks UPHELD, a THIRD latent bug ADDED). 07c REPRODUCES VERBATIM: (1) qty quirk — bill.qty=chart.qty with amount/balance computed at price*qty on the reachable admission path, while PatientInvoiceDetail.qty is hard-coded 1 at all four sites (PatientServiceImpl.java:2372/2388/2441/2457), each carrying invoice-detail.amount = full patientBill.getAmount() and description 'Consumable: <name>' (money-neutral; verified that savePatientConsumableChart is reachable only for the admission path so one of the two amount-setting branches always overwrites the single-unit price before persistence). (2) Mislabeled ref — delete_consumable_chart sets patientCreditNote.setReference('Canceled lab test') (PatientResource.java:3053, copy-paste from the lab-delete path) on a consumable credit note, raised only when a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists, at full bill amount. CORRECTION APPLIED — THIRD LATENT BUG (verified at PatientResource.java:3070-3076): the delete-path parent-invoice cleanup uses 'int j=0; for(detail : invoice.getPatientInvoiceDetails()){ j=j++; } if(j==0) delete invoice' — j=j++ is a Java no-op leaving j ALWAYS 0, so the parent PatientInvoice is deleted UNCONDITIONALLY, never 'only if empty'; PatientInvoice declares @OneToMany(fetch=EAGER, orphanRemoval=true) so deleting the parent CASCADE-WIPES every sibling PatientInvoiceDetail (e.g. a shared PENDING invoice's consultation/lab lines). 07c MUST reproduce the UNCONDITIONAL parent-invoice deletion verbatim (delete the parent PatientInvoice after deleting this consumable's detail, regardless of remaining siblings), with a golden-master asserting a shared multi-detail PENDING invoice is fully removed; MUST NOT replace it with a real emptiness check. 07c MUST NOT (absent approved CR-07-Q11): set invoiceDetail.qty=chart.qty, normalize qty across branches, relabel the credit-note reference, or add a real emptiness check. QA asserts invoiceDetail.qty==1, creditNote.reference=='Canceled lab test', and the cascade-wipe as positive expectations with code comments + CR cross-reference flagging all THREE as preserved-latent-bugs. CR-07-Q11 now bundles all three suspected latent bugs for owner sign-off; legacy-analyst confirms intent (input to the CR only; baseline reproduces verbatim regardless). adrCitation corrected to ADR-0008 by its real title 'Bounded-Context Decomposition & Module Boundaries' (its recordClinicalCharge command, §line 69, ChargeRequest with NO qty/billItem field, is silent on invoice-detail qty and credit-note text; Proposed/unratified, pre-decides nothing).

RATIFIED AS DECIDED (challenge UPHELD). EXACT-PROCESS BASELINE = billing-only. 07c reproduces legacy consumable issue verbatim: save_patient_consumable_chart creates a PatientConsumableChart + accrues a PatientBill via billing::api recordClinicalCharge (3-way outcome UNPAID cash | COVERED via MedicineInsurancePlan + PatientInvoiceDetail | VERIFIED cash-on-admission), with the qty=1 invoice-detail quirk (Q11), the Consumable-registration guard ('Medicine is not listed as consumable'), and the admission-status gate (PENDING rejects 'Admission not verified'; IN-PROCESS accepts; else 'already signed off'). It touches NO stock entity, decrements NO inc-08 lot, writes NO stock-card row; delete raises a credit note + cascade with NO stock restoration (verified PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475 returns with zero Store/StoreStock/Medicine-qty/ledger touch). The named dependency ConsumableStockBalance.decrementForIssue is a CONFIRMED PHANTOM (zero source matches; lives only in docs). 07c MUST NOT build a CONSUMABLE_ISSUE decrement seam, promote StockService/StoreStockService.decrementFefo to @NamedInterface for inpatient, reuse PharmacyStockCredit.debitTransferOut (writes a transfer-flavoured TRANSFER_OUT card, semantically wrong), restore stock on delete, or build the D5 last-unit-409 concurrency AC (REMOVED — there is no stock contention on a billing-only path). 'Consumables draw from stock' is the net-new improvement PARKED as CR-07-consumable-stock. If APPROVED later: a follow-on architecture task (engagement-lead -> data-architect + inventory/pharmacy owners) designs a non-transfer-flavoured CONSUMABLE_ISSUE @NamedInterface decrement seam writing an ISSUE/CONSUMPTION-classified card, and the inc-08 parked @Version-only concurrency posture (CR-08-Q4/Q3) must be revisited (the new path reintroduces last-unit contention). ServiceKind for the consumable charge is decided in Q13.

RATIFIED WITH CORRECTION APPLIED + RECLASSIFIED (challenge REVISE — pricing-kind choice UPHELD; four corrections applied; classification changed from ARCHITECTURE_DECISION to OWNER_RELEASE_GATE on the owner-routing correction). PRICING-KIND CHOICE (unchanged, recommended): charge inpatient consumables under the EXISTING ServiceKind.MEDICINE; do NOT add ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE. PriceLookup.resolve(planUid, MEDICINE, medicineUid, ccy) reproduces the legacy path exactly (cash=Medicine.getPrice() PatientServiceImpl.java:2308; insurance via medicineInsurancePlanRepository.findByMedicineAndInsurancePlanAndCovered :2323/2326-2327 — which is what MEDICINE keys to, ServiceKind.java:37 -> medicine_insurance_plans by Medicine.uid). Freeze the 7-kind enum and both CHECK constraints (ck_service_prices_kind V9:110-111, ck_patient_bills_kind V15:73-75) at REGISTRATION/CONSULTATION/LAB_TEST/MEDICINE/PROCEDURE/RADIOLOGY/WARD; no schema change for the pricing kind. Adding ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE would force a net-new pricing kind + dual CHECK migration for zero behavioural benefit — explicitly NOT pursued. CORRECTIONS APPLIED (verified at source): (A) DROP the false claim that the charge sets ChargeRequest.billItem/description — those fields DO NOT EXIST on the as-built ChargeRequest record (ChargeRequest.java:27-37) and recordClinicalCharge has no caller-supplied billItem/description (BillingChargeService derives billItem=labelFor(kind) line 115, description=billItem line 116). (B) BLOCKING 07c build item: the legacy bill-line display identity billItem='Medication' / description='Consumable: <medicineName>' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2312-2313) is NOT reproducible through recordClinicalCharge unchanged — it would emit billItem='Medicine' (labelFor(MEDICINE), BillingChargeService.java:276) and description='Medicine' (line 116). Reproducing the legacy literals is verified DISPLAY DRIFT requiring a billing-API extension (extend ChargeRequest/recordClinicalCharge with billItem+description, or route consumables through a seam that accepts them) — it is NOT zero-build-impact. (C) GOLDEN-MASTER RECONCILIATION required for the cash-UNPAID consumable bill amount: legacy stores amount=unit price (Medicine.getPrice(), :2308) with balance=price*qty (:2310), while as-built computeAmount(MEDICINE) yields amount=price*qty (BillingChargeService.java:260-265); 07c must verify the resulting PatientBill amount/balance reproduce the legacy values at 2dp, not merely assert identity. (D) RE-ROUTE: the inc-07 discovery doc routes Q13 to the engagement-lead (01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md:110), so MEDICINE is PRESENTED as the recommended placement but ownerActionRequired=engagement-lead confirmation; do not self-close an owner-routed question. N14 qty=1 invoiceDetail quirk and N11/N16 mislabeled credit-note reference remain parked under Q11; consumables-from-stock baseline remains billing-only under Q12.

changeRequests: CR-07-MAR (Q1) — Add a net-new closed-loop MedicationAdministration/MAR aggregate (routeUid, administeredAt, doseGiven, patientResponse) + route masterdata + closed-loop administration endpoint as a clinical-safety enhancement over the legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing note. NET-NEW (zero legacy basis; M15 aggregate and its dispensedAt rationale are confirmed phantom). Requires owner sign-off + clinical-safety justification; if approved, additionally needs route/administration masterdata (data-architect) and a PHI/audit decision (security-architect). MAR remains a net-new acceptance test only, NOT a golden-master parity item, until approved. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-Q2 (Q2) — Adopt net-new @Scheduled+ShedLock ward-day accrual cron (calendar-night model) in place of the legacy rolling-24h-from-openedAt polling Thread, accepting the documented midnight-vs-rolling-24h timing variance. Pending ADR-0018 ratification + scheduling-infra authorization (ShedLock dependency, shedlock + job_run_log Flyway tables, @EnableScheduling). UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-Q3 (Q3) — Pessimistic (PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) row lock on the WardBed/Admission bed-claim aggregate (extends parked CR-08-Q4 to the inpatient aggregate). MUST bundle as an explicit dependency the net-new shared-kernel STALE_ENTITY surfacing (add ErrorCode.STALE_ENTITY urn:hmis:error:stale-entity 409 + @ExceptionHandler(ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException.class) in GlobalExceptionHandler — both currently absent and parked behind CR-08-Q4/ADR-0017). Raise only if owner elects the deviation; gated on ADR-0017 ratification + explicit owner deviation approval (ADR-0017 alone does not authorize a bed extension). UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-SoD (Q4) — Net-new second-approver Segregation-of-Duties gate on discharge/disposition approval (approvedBy != createdBy). Bundles: (1) approve the SoD deviation; (2) IAM operation-vocabulary decision (APPROVE-suffixed privilege only, e.g. DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE; *-DISCHARGE/*-WRITE are purge-incompatible per MainApplication.java:316-339 vs security/Operation.java); (3) net-new SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN ErrorCode. Forward-guard: any deceased-path touch must reuse PatientDeceasedEvent and add no boolean/admit-guard/PATIENT_DECEASED. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-deceased-guard (Q5) — Add admit-time (and OPD-booking) deceased-patient block + PATIENT_DECEASED RFC 7807 error type. NET-NEW clinical-safety guard, no legacy basis (closes the residual gap where a signed-out, now-DECEASED patient is re-admittable). MUST NOT introduce a Patient.deceased boolean (CR-05). UNAPPROVED/parked, HDE + engagement-lead decision.

CR-INC07-Q7 (Q7) — Build the referral external_medical_providers masterdata (entity + seed + CRUD) and convert ReferralPlan.external_medical_provider_uid to a real validated reference. Scoped to BOTH clinical (V28) and inpatient referral plans for consistency. At raise-time, reconcile ADR-0022's 'Accepted' self-status against the README 'Proposed' register. UNAPPROVED/parked (data-architect-owned default keeps loose uid now).

CR-07-ward-transfer (Q8) — Add net-new mid-stay ward/bed transfer (entity + endpoint + transfer state + billing/insurance re-anchoring + audit). ACs MUST be authored as net-new requirements, NOT framed as reproducing legacy behaviour (no legacy ward transfer exists). UNAPPROVED/parked pending engagement-lead phase-gate funding decision.

CR-07-Q9 (Q9) — Honour WardTypeInsurancePlan.active in insurance ward-price eligibility (exclude inactive-but-covered plans from the selection loop). Claims-integrity hardening; deviation from legacy active-flag-ignored behaviour. HDE recommends APPROVE (inactive coverage tier leaking into NHIF ward claims is a real submission-correctness risk) but it is the owner's call. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-Q10 (Q10) — Close the final AdmissionBed at discharge/sign-out (set status='CLOSED' + closedAt) to fix the legacy open-ledger leak. DEVIATION from legacy (which leaves it OPENED); keep WardBed/AdmissionBed distinct. Optional cleanup; does NOT gate the baseline. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-Q11 (Q11) — Correct THREE suspected latent legacy bugs in consumable issue/delete: (1) populate PatientInvoiceDetail.qty with chart.qty (instead of hard-coded 1); (2) set the consumable PatientCreditNote.reference to consumable-specific text (instead of the mislabeled 'Canceled lab test'); (3) replace the unconditional parent-PatientInvoice deletion (the j=j++ no-op bug at PatientResource.java:3070-3076 that cascade-wipes sibling invoice details via orphanRemoval) with a real emptiness check. Deferred fix; owner-approved; golden-master re-baseline (incl. the cascade-wipe expectation) on approval. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-consumable-stock (Q12) — Make inpatient consumable issue decrement inc-08 stock (NET-NEW vs legacy billing-only). Introduces a new non-transfer-flavoured CONSUMABLE_ISSUE @NamedInterface decrement seam over StockService/StoreStockService writing an ISSUE/CONSUMPTION-classified stock-card row (NOT TRANSFER_OUT) and re-enables a last-unit concurrency AC; if approved, the inc-08 parked @Version-only posture (CR-08-Q4/Q3) must be revisited. UNAPPROVED/parked.

CR-07-Q13-billing-display (Q13, conditional) — Extend the billing API so the consumable charge can reproduce the legacy bill-line display identity billItem='Medication' / description='Consumable: <medicineName>' (add billItem+description to ChargeRequest/recordClinicalCharge, or a seam that accepts them). Required ONLY because the as-built recordClinicalCharge emits billItem/description='Medicine' for MEDICINE-kind — verified display drift. This is a real (non-zero) build-impact item that must be raised before 07c can reproduce the legacy literals; it does NOT change the pricing-kind decision (MEDICINE).
ownerActionsRequired: Q1 / CR-07-MAR: engagement-lead (with HDE clinical-safety input) decides whether to approve and fund the net-new closed-loop MAR feature in a later increment. Until sign-off, MAR stays parked and the exact-process baseline (legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing-note path) is the build of record. Not required for 07a/07b/07c to proceed.

Q2 / ward accrual: engagement-lead + data-architect must (1) RATIFY ADR-0018 — but ONLY AFTER the solution-architect issues the upstream correction to ADR-0018 replacing the false 'one flat charge, does not re-accrue' premise; (2) AUTHORIZE net-new scheduling infrastructure (ShedLock dependency, shedlock + job_run_log Flyway migrations, @EnableScheduling); (3) DECIDE the parity bar — accept the midnight-cron-vs-rolling-24h timing variance as a tolerated, runbook-documented deviation (approve CR-07-Q2) OR require true elapsed-24h parity (forcing a rolling-interval design). All three must land before 07c codes the accrual scheduler; the accrual service method + parity oracle proceed now.

Q3 / bed-claim lock: engagement-lead ONLY — decide whether to lift the lock for the bed/admission aggregate, i.e. approve CR-07-Q3 (the deviation) AND ratify ADR-0017 (both required; ADR-0017's pessimistic scope is stock/documents so a bed extension needs an explicit CR even post-ratification). The bundled net-new STALE_ENTITY surfacing (ErrorCode + OptimisticLock handler) is part of this gate and must NOT be built into the 07a baseline before it. Until both land, the @Version-only baseline is frozen and 07a proceeds on it.

Q4 / CR-07-SoD: engagement-lead must rule on the bundle — (a) approve/reject the net-new SoD deviation; (b) make the IAM operation-vocabulary decision (APPROVE-suffixed object split only; *-DISCHARGE/*-WRITE are purge-incompatible) and decide how any new code seeds against the legacy 35-code IAM baseline; (c) authorize the net-new SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN ErrorCode. security-architect recommends but does not decide or pre-name the privilege. Not required for the 07a single-actor baseline to proceed.

Q5 / CR-07-deceased-guard: engagement-lead (with HDE input) decides approve-as-net-new (build the guard + PATIENT_DECEASED type + IAM/error-catalogue additions) vs reproduce-legacy-as-is (accept the residual re-admit gap for parity). Do not build either way until the decision lands; the legacy-faithful baseline (no admit-time deceased branch) proceeds now.

Q9 / CR-07-Q9: engagement-lead must rule on the active-flag fix (HDE recommends APPROVE as low-risk claims-integrity hardening). Absent approval the baseline reproduces the quirk. No owner sign-off is needed to build the top-up split or the selection loop — those are mandatory exact-process 07a build items regardless of the CR outcome.

Q10 / CR-07-Q10: engagement-lead may OPTIONALLY approve closing AdmissionBed at discharge. This is the only owner action for Q10 and it is optional — it gates the deviation, not the baseline. Without approval, 07a/07c ship the verbatim leak.

Q11 / CR-07-Q11: engagement-lead is the phase-gate approver for fixing the three suspected latent bugs (qty=1, mislabeled credit-note reference, unconditional parent-invoice cascade-delete). legacy-analyst confirms intent (deliberate vs latent) as CR input only. Until approved, the verbatim-reproduction baseline (including the cascade-wipe) stands.

Q12 / CR-07-consumable-stock: engagement-lead approves or declines funding the net-new 'consumables draw from stock' improvement (deviation sign-off only; does NOT block the 07c billing-only baseline). If approved, a follow-on architecture task routes via engagement-lead to data-architect + inventory/pharmacy module owners to design the new CONSUMABLE_ISSUE seam, and the inc-08 @Version-only concurrency posture (CR-08-Q4/Q3) must be revisited.

Q13 / ServiceKind for consumables: engagement-lead CONFIRMATION required (Q13 is owner-routed per 01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md:110). MEDICINE is the recommended placement (no schema change, no ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE). Bundled into this confirmation: acknowledge that reproducing the legacy bill-line literals billItem='Medication'/description='Consumable: <name>' requires the billing-API extension CR-07-Q13-billing-display (a real, non-zero build item), and that 07c must golden-master-reconcile the cash-UNPAID amount quirk (legacy amount=unit price vs as-built amount=price*qty) at 2dp.

register.baselineScopeEffect: NET EFFECT ON 07a/07b/07c EXACT-PROCESS BASELINE (IN-baseline vs parked-as-CR/owner-gated). 07a (Admission + Discharge + Ward billing) IN: AdmissionBed ledger (status OPENED/CLOSED CHECK-constrained, openedAt NOT NULL, closedAt NULL, @OneToOne FK to ward-bed PatientBill, non-updatable FKs) created OPENED at admit and LEFT OPENED at discharge (Q10 verbatim leak); admission-time UNPAID ward-bed bill at WardType.price (qty=1); WardTypeInsurancePlan max-price/exact-plan-match selection checking ONLY price+plan-id (active flag carried but IGNORED, Q9) producing the COVERED principal bill + the LOAD-BEARING UNPAID 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' supplementary bill and its activation-gate wiring (no-top-up flips IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED; top-up leaves PENDING/WAITING); the open-admission re-admit hard-reject on {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} with the verbatim message -> BUSINESS_RULE 422 (Q5); the legacy single-actor disposition approval approvedBy=createdBy with PENDING->APPROVED only, three disposition entities, AuditRecorder who/when capture (Q4); the inpatient deceased terminal publishing the EXISTING PatientDeceasedEvent to flip PatientType=DECEASED via the registration listener (Q6), freeing WardBed=EMPTY at HELD without closing AdmissionBed; the inpatient ReferralPlan reusing the clinical referral_plans table with a mandatory loose external_medical_provider_uid, no FK (Q7); WardBed cycle EMPTY->WAITING->OCCUPIED->EMPTY guarded by @Version (Q3, first-commit-wins, no double-claim). AdmissionStatus FROZEN at PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT. 07b (Nursing charts) IN: the legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing-note write path (GIVEN-prescription guard, exactly-one encounter binding, admission-IN-PROCESS gate, nurse-uid requirement) + the clinical::api read surface + the 24h DELETE guard (delete-only; no edit path) (Q1). 07c (Consumable + ward accrual) IN: billing-only consumable issue via recordClinicalCharge 3-way outcome (UNPAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), invoiceDetail.qty=1 verbatim + bill.qty=chart.qty (Q11), Consumable-registration guard, admission-status gate, delete with credit-note (mislabeled reference verbatim) + UNCONDITIONAL parent-invoice cascade-delete verbatim (Q11) + NO stock restoration (Q12); the consumable charge priced under ServiceKind.MEDICINE (Q13 recommended, pending owner confirmation); the ward-accrual TOTAL/STATUS parity semantics (1+N)xWardType.price as the oracle, with the accrueWardDay service method stageable but NO scheduler binding. PARKED (CR / owner-gated, MUST NOT be built into the baseline): MedicationAdministration/MAR aggregate + route masterdata + closed-loop endpoint (CR-07-MAR); @Scheduled+ShedLock accrual cron + shedlock/job_run_log tables + @EnableScheduling (CR-07-Q2, gated on ADR-0018); PESSIMISTIC_WRITE bed-claim lock + the net-new STALE_ENTITY ErrorCode/OptimisticLock handler surfacing (CR-07-Q3, gated on ADR-0017); second-approver SoD gate + SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN + APPROVE-suffixed privilege (CR-07-SoD); admit-time deceased guard + PATIENT_DECEASED (CR-07-deceased-guard); external_medical_providers masterdata + real referral FK (CR-INC07-Q7); WardTransfer entity/endpoint/TRANSFERRED status (CR-07-ward-transfer); active-flag eligibility fix (CR-07-Q9); close-AdmissionBed-at-discharge (CR-07-Q10); the three consumable latent-bug fixes (CR-07-Q11); consumables-draw-from-stock + CONSUMABLE_ISSUE seam + last-unit-409 AC (CR-07-consumable-stock); ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE + dual CHECK migration (explicitly NOT pursued). REMOVED ACs: the D5 last-unit-409 consumable concurrency test (no stock contention on a billing-only path); the inc-07-plan 'deceased guard' golden-master PARITY framing (reclassified — no legacy basis); the bed-claim '409 STALE_ENTITY' concurrency assertion (downgraded to the no-double-claim correctness invariant). adrCorrections: ADR-0018 (Background Jobs and Scheduling), §Context line 15 and §Exact-process-impact lines 130-132: the premise 'legacy charges one flat ward-bed bill at admission and does not re-accrue' is FACTUALLY WRONG and MUST be corrected by the solution-architect BEFORE ADR-0018 is ratified. Verified legacy ground truth: UpdatePatient.java (a Runnable started at MainApplication.java:160-184) polls every ~5 minutes (Thread.sleep 300000, line 110) and for each IN-PROCESS/STOPPED admission (lines 258-262), when ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(currentBed.openedAt, now) >= 24 (lines 291-292), CLOSES the current AdmissionBed (status=CLOSED, closedAt set) and OPENS a new AdmissionBed with openedAt=now (lines 294-334) plus a new VERIFIED ward-bed PatientBill at WardType.price (lines 304-325). So legacy DOES re-accrue per CHAINED rolling-24h windows (each accrual resets openedAt=now, line 333). Replace the false premise with the verified rolling-24h re-accrual fact; ADR-0018 JOB-001's golden-master must reconcile against the legacy elapsed-24h-from-openedAt chained total, NOT a calendar-night count, and must model the chained-reset-plus-5-minute-poll-latency mechanic (the chained count N can be strictly lower than floor(elapsed/24h) for long stays).

ADR-0018 self-labelling note (no change to the label, recorded for ratification context): §Exact-process-impact lines 130-132 correctly self-label JOB-001's calendar-night cron MECHANISM as 'a deliberate process improvement, not a divergence' / NET-NEW. After the §Context premise correction above, the distinction is sharpened: the accrual TOTAL/STATUS semantics are EXTRACTED legacy parity facts (not net-new), while only the trigger MECHANISM (@Scheduled+ShedLock cron) and the per-calendar-night idempotency model are net-new — the corrected ADR must keep that separation explicit.

Its substantive content (the recordClinicalCharge / ChargeRequest command at ~line 69, which carries patientUid/chargeType/amount/paymentType/insurancePlanUid and NO qty/billItem/description field) is SILENT on invoice-detail quantity and credit-note reference text, and ADR-0008 is itself only Proposed/unratified (README line 3), so it pre-decides nothing for Q11/Q13.

This internal inconsistency must be reconciled before any CR (e.g. CR-INC07-Q7) is built on an ADR-0022 assumption; the Q7 ruling deliberately does not depend on ADR-0022's status (it rests on merged V28/V37 engineering precedent), so no immediate change is forced, but the engagement-lead should resolve the status drift in the ADR register.
